assertions.notes: When the card catalog is populated (test-data.sql has 20 set_cards), DeckList resolves card names from metadata. When the catalog is empty, getCardName() falls back to 'Unknown Card {id}' and the element renders with data-testid='unknown-card'. This assertion catches the 'Unknown Card' regression: if set_cards is empty (failed ingest), every card in the deck shows 'Unknown Card'. The seeded DSK deck (deck-004) has 5 card types that exist in test-data.sql.

Field-level correctness for the most-used card metadata. 'No Unknown Card' only catches an empty catalog; it does NOT catch a card that resolves but has a blank mana cost or a wrong rarity gem. A player sees mana cost and rarity on every card in the deck view every session — these are the highest-visibility data fields. Each card in the corpus deck must have: (1) mana_cost non-empty string (any valid mana expression including '0' or '{0}' for zero-cost cards); (2) rarity one of the four canonical values; (3) color_identity present and non-empty (colorless cards use ['C'] per Scryfall convention — a completely missing or empty color_identity means the ingest did not write the field). This assertion is driven by the actual /api/v1/cards wire response for the five seeded card IDs.

rarity.rule: must be one of: common, uncommon, rare, mythic

color_identity.rule: must be present and non-empty array
color_identity.note: colorless cards use ['C'] — an empty array means the ingest omitted the field
